name: Sprawling organic silhouette
description: Overall silhouette of a dwelling whose footprint reads as 3 to 5 rectangular volumes of unequal scale, meeting at irregular angles, jogs, or short stepped offsets rather than resolving into a single geometric shape. The composition simulates organic growth: a primary main-house volume anchors the plan, secondary wings attach perpendicular or askew, and one or two smaller additions (scullery, mudroom, lean-to) cling to the perimeter at different heights. Roof pitches and ridge directions vary per volume, producing a broken, accreted skyline characteristic of medieval town houses, English country manors, and old rustic farmsteads grown by generations of inhabitants. Consumed by the global_designer to seed an irregular envelope before space_planner subdivides interiors per volume.
placement_rules: Compose the footprint from 3 to 5 rectangular volumes of distinctly different scales; avoid making any two volumes identical in size or aspect.
Designate the largest rectangle as the main volume; attach secondary wings either perpendicular to it, at small angular offsets, or shifted by 1-3 block jogs along the shared edge.
Vary storey count between volumes: the main volume is 2 storeys, at least one secondary wing is 1 storey to break the skyline.
Give each volume its own roof: combine gabled, hipped, or shed pitches so ridges run in different directions and meet at irregular junctions.
Place the main entrance on the facade of the largest volume, never at a junction between volumes.
Small additions (lean-to, scullery, mudroom) should sit at lower height than the volume they attach to and may share a wall rather than stand free.
Avoid mirror symmetry of the global outline; the silhouette must read as accreted over time, not designed in one stroke.

medieval.notes: Timber-frame wings on cobblestone bases; each volume's roof in dark wood at a different pitch; jettied upper storey on the main volume.

victorian.notes: Brick main block with stone-trimmed bay wings; tall narrow chimneys mark each volume; steep slate-look roofs.

rustic.notes: Weathered timber farmstead grown by generations: shed-roofed lean-tos in spruce, mossy stone footings, low eaves on the smaller volumes.
